rationale: The citing paper uses Chang and Hung (2018) in its core theory section as specific evidence that private self-focus makes consumers act on inner feelings, not just as a generic background citation. This prior finding is used to support the proposed psychological chain that smartphone use heightens private self-focus, which then increases preference for unique and self-expressive options. Although the citation appears within a broader literature review, it plays a clear conceptual setup role in motivating the mechanism tested in the paper.
relation_description: Song and Sela treat Chang and Hung (2018) as prior evidence on downstream effects of private self-focus in consumer decisions. They build their framework on that base by proposing a new antecedent (personal smartphone use) and a different consequence (uniqueness seeking).
